intro: Washington's overnight ban on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominates — the first use of US export controls on a deployed AI model. Elsewhere: AI CEOs head to the G7, Cognition remakes the IDE, and Bitcoin's 13-day ETF outflow streak snaps.

items.headline: US export controls force Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.summary: A Commerce Department directive delivered June 12 at 5:21 pm ET ordered Anthropic to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for any foreign national — inside or outside the US, including its own non-citizen staff. Anthropic disabled both models globally; Opus 4.8 and all other Claude models remain fully available.
items.why: The first time US export controls have been applied to a live, deployed AI model — a precedent every frontier lab will be watching.

items.headline: Anthropic disputes the shutdown, warns of industry-wide chilling effect
items.summary: The order followed a claimed jailbreak of Mythos 5. Anthropic's review found only minor previously known vulnerabilities and says comparable behavior can be elicited from public models including GPT-5.5; applying the same standard broadly would halt all new model deployments.
items.why: If a single demonstrated jailbreak can trigger a global suspension, every frontier lab's release calculus changes.

items.headline: Altman, Amodei, and Hassabis confirmed for G7 summit in France, June 15–17
items.summary: French President Macron confirmed that OpenAI's Sam Altman, Anthropic's Dario Amodei, and Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis will attend the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains — the first time the three rivals will appear together at a major geopolitical gathering. AI infrastructure, regulation, and youth safety are on the agenda.
items.why: The conversation between governments and AI labs is moving from advisory panels to the same table as G7 heads of state.

items.headline: Cognition relaunches Windsurf as Devin Desktop — an agent-neutral IDE with ACP support
items.summary: Cognition shipped an OTA update on June 2 that rebranded Windsurf as Devin Desktop: a coding environment with a built-in Agent Command Center that orchestrates local and cloud agents via the open Agent Client Protocol. At launch it supports Codex, Claude Agent, and OpenCode alongside Devin.
items.why: ACP could become the USB-C of agentic coding — if it sticks, no single vendor owns the agent layer in your IDE.

items.headline: OpenAI Codex Goal Mode reaches general availability
items.summary: Codex's /goal command graduated from beta to GA across the web app, IDE extension, and CLI. A goal sets a persistent completion condition that lets Codex run multi-hour plan-act-test loops — writing code, running tests, and patching failures — without further prompting.
items.why: Raises the floor for what enterprises expect from agentic coding; competitors now have to match autonomous, long-running task execution.

items.headline: Fable 5 shutdown forces dev teams to find fallback models overnight
items.summary: With Fable 5 and Mythos 5 suspended globally by government order, any team that migrated agentic or long-context workloads to those models needs an immediate continuity plan. Anthropic confirmed Opus 4.8 and all other Claude models remain available.
items.why: A live reminder that single-model dependency is now a real availability risk, not just a pricing concern.

items.headline: Xcode 27 ships with on-device agentic coding — no cloud API bill required
items.summary: Apple unveiled Xcode 27 at WWDC on June 9: Apple silicon–only, 30% smaller, and bundled with agentic coding that runs top-tier models from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI locally via the new Core AI framework. The Foundation Models API offers free Private Cloud Compute access for developers with under two million App Store downloads.
items.why: Apple is making AI-assisted coding a default, zero-marginal-cost feature of its platform — resetting expectations for every dev tool that charges per token.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot switches to token-metered billing; agentic users report bills jumping from $39 to $800+/mo
items.summary: As of June 1, Copilot replaced Premium Request Units with AI Credits (1 credit = $0.01). Code completions stay unlimited, but chat, agentic sessions, and code review now consume credits that do not roll over month to month. Heavy agentic users are reporting steep cost increases.
items.why: Worth auditing your team's token spend before the June billing cycle closes.

items.headline: .NET June 2026 servicing patches two active CVEs across .NET 8, 9, and 10
items.summary: Microsoft shipped .NET 10.0.9, 9.0.17, and 8.0.28 on June 9, addressing CVE-2026-45491 and CVE-2026-45490 in ASP.NET Core and the .NET runtime. No .NET Framework security updates were included this cycle.
items.why: Both CVEs are actively tracked; update before month-end if you haven't already.

items.headline: Bitcoin's 13-day ETF outflow streak snaps — $85.85M net inflow on June 12
items.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETF flows turned positive on June 12 with $85.85M in net inflows, ending a 13-session bleeding streak that drained $4.37B in total. Bitcoin was trading around $64,100 with an oversold RSI near 35; roughly 75% of prior outflows traced to BlackRock's IBIT.
items.why: The streak ending doesn't confirm a bottom, but it does close the most sustained institutional de-risking since spot ETFs launched.

items.headline: Strategy sells 32 BTC — first sale since 2022 — to cover preferred dividends
items.summary: Michael Saylor's Strategy disclosed in early June that it sold 32 BTC in late May for $2.5 million — the firm's first Bitcoin disposal since December 2022 — to fund STRK preferred stock dividends. MSTR shares fell 25% and BTC dropped roughly 15% in the weeks after the disclosure.
items.why: When the most iconic corporate bitcoin hodler sells — even symbolically — markets overreact; the 15% BTC drop shows how outsized Saylor's hold on the narrative remains.

items.headline: ARMA bill proposes 1M BTC strategic reserve with 20-year lock-up and quarterly proof of reserves
items.summary: Reps. Nick Begich and Jared Golden introduced the American Reserve Modernization Act on May 21, seeking to codify the executive-order Strategic Bitcoin Reserve into statute, authorize Treasury to acquire up to 200,000 BTC annually for five years, and mandate quarterly Proof of Reserve audits. All related bills remain stalled in committee.
items.why: The bill stalling in committee is itself the news: no statutory backstop for the reserve exists yet, leaving the whole program reversible by executive action.
